channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: School choice, vouchers and the future of Texas education
item.description: Everything you need to know about what they are and how they might change the state’s educational landscape.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, Ai Tools By Suraj Thapa, Graphics By Yuriko Schumacher, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump taps Sean Curran as next Secret Service director
item.description: Curran was part of Trump's security details during an attempted assassination in Butler, Pennsylvania, last July.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration, according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's ICE racks up hundreds of arrests, including illegal immigrants arrested for horror crimes
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement, in the first days of the Trump administration, has made hundreds of arrests of illegal immigrants across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pardons former DC police officers convicted in death of man during deadly pursuit
item.description: Andrew Zabavsky and Terence Sutton Jr. were pardoned by President Donald Trump. The Washington, D.C., police officers were convicted in the 2020 death of a man during a police chase.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Day One actions reversed Biden-era health policies, including efforts to expand ObamaCare
item.description: President Trump is rolling back Biden healthcare policies, such as expansions to the Affordable Care Act – a move Democrats described as an "attack" on the federal program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s threat of tariffs and sanctions on Russia over Ukraine may fall flat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened on Wednesday to impose stiff taxes, tariffs and sanctions on Russia if an agreement isn’t reached to end the war in Ukraine, a warning that is likely fall on deaf ears in the Kremlin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace says she is considering a run for South Carolina governor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina said Wednesday that she is “seriously considering” a gubernatorial run in 2026 and will make a final decision in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, the far-right extremist group leader convicted of seditious conspiracy in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack, has visited Capitol Hill after President Donald Trump commuted his 18-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs and sanctions if it does not agree to end war
item.description: It is unclear how much impact economic penalties would have on the Russian government, since they already face various sanctions imposed by the previous administration.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde at the inaugural prayer service.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson won’t ‘second-guess’ Trump pardons of violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) defended President Trump’s sweeping pardons of those who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, including numerous people who violently attacked police officers. “The president's made his decision. I don't second-guess those,” Johnson said at a press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pardons by Trump and Biden reveal distrust of each other and wobbly faith in criminal justice system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A day that began with the outgoing president's pardon of lawmakers and his own family ended with the incoming president's pardon of supporters who violently stormed the Capitol four years ago.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New allegations against Pete Hegseth alarm Democrats as Pentagon confirmation looms
item.description: Senate Democrats are sounding the alarm over a sworn affidavit from the ex-sister-in-law of Defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth that accuses him of being abusive toward his second wife.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday demanded an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she made a direct appeal to him during a prayer service marking his inauguration to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrant workers who are in the United States illegally.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, TIFFANY STANLEY and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans 'full steam ahead' on Trump's Cabinet picks
item.description: The possibility of recess appointments, which would allow some nominees to be confirmed without a vote, is on the table.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrats stick to work closer to home as row with Republicans persists at Minnesota Capitol
item.description: Democrats in the Minnesota House are boycotting the Capitol over a disagreement about the power Republicans should hold given a temporary advantage there. They’re doing events in their districts instead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
